reasoning: Two views shown (4x left, 8x right). Pixel boundaries are generally clean square blocks with no sub-pixel features or AA smearing — passes hard-edge test. Background is solid dark with no noise. However: (1) Cyan/teal pixels appear scattered at the periphery of the right explosion — anomalous hue injection inconsistent with fire/orange palette, classic AI hallucination of palette colors. (2) Orange tones span ~5 distinct values (dark-brown, mid-orange, bright-orange, yellow-orange, yellow) suggesting gradient shading compressed into pixel form rather than a strict 2-3 tone limited palette. (3) Semantic failure: cyan sparks in a fire explosion are not physically motivated and suggest the generator confused heat-plasma with electric arc imagery. Explosion silhouette is readable and symmetric enough to be plausible but asymmetry distribution looks slightly mechanical. No melting or outline inconsistency detected. Net: clean technical execution but AI color contamination and over-graduated orange palette drop this to marginal.
